articles.title: UK borrowed a higher than expected £24.3bn in April
articles.summary: Figure comes as Middle East conflict and political turmoil darken outlook for the public finances

articles.title: Legal disputes rip through oil shipping after Hormuz closure
articles.summary: TotalEnergies weighs lawsuit against Baltic Exchange, following Mercuria, as shipping derivative losses mount

articles.title: China Scrutinizes Companies, Funds After AI-Fueled Stock Moves
articles.summary: Chinese regulators are scrutinizing recent stock rallies that have been fueled by artificial intelligence optimism, asking some listed companies and funds to give more details about their approach to the technology, according to people familiar with the matter.

articles.title: Japan to Receive First Oil Tanker to Exit Hormuz Since War Began
articles.summary: Japan — one of Asia’s biggest importers of energy from the Middle East — flagged the impending arrival of the first Persian Gulf oil shipment to transit the contested Strait of Hormuz since the Iran war began.

articles.title: Shell Returns to Dutch Supreme Court in Landmark Climate Case
articles.summary: Supermajor Shell is facing the Netherlands’ Supreme Court in an emissions lawsuit that a few years ago led to a ruling forcing the company to slash emissions from operations by 45% by 2030, which was later overturned at a higher court. Back in 2021, the District Court in The Hague ordered Shell to reduce its carbon dioxide emissions by 45% by 2030 in a landmark ruling on a suit brought in front of the court by environmentalist group Milieudefensie, other NGOs, and a group of private individuals. At the time, commentators said that the ruling…
